mnemonics: Saints Take Everything, Keeping Lots Of Dollars Yearly
Smartest Twin Always Keeps Larger Goods Yearly
keyPoints: The Stackelberg oligopoly model describes a situation where one firm (the leader) sets its price before the other firm (the follower) sets its price.
The follower firm will set its price lower than the leader, as it anticipates the leader's price and wants to capture as much of the market as possible.
The leader firm will earn a higher profit than the follower firm, as it gets to set the price and capture the entire market.
